qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via forwardProxy permits unauthenticated arbitrary outbound HTTP requests
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an SSRF vulnerability in the /api/network/forwardProxy endpoint, while the advisory describes a path traversal vulnerability in the /export/ endpoint due to double URL encoding. Different vulnerability classes (SSRF vs path traversal), different endpoints (/api/network/forwardProxy vs /export/), different code locations (kernel/api/network.go vs kernel/server/serve.go:314-320), and different root causes (unauthenticated forward proxy vs redundant url.PathUnescape call). While both affect the same repository (SiYuan), they are distinct vulnerabilities.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes a path traversal vulnerability in the /export/ endpoint (kernel/server/serve.go:314-320) caused by a redundant url.PathUnescape() call that allows double URL encoding (%252e%252e) to bypass the CVE-2026-30869 fix. The single qualifying finding is an SSRF vulnerability in the forwardProxy endpoint (forwardproxy-ssrf), which describes a different vulnerability class, different endpoint, different code location, and different root cause than the advisory-described path traversal vulnerability.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-41894: Path traversal in /export/ endpoint via double URL encoding in kernel/server/serve.go:314-320
The finding does not match the vulnerable /export/ endpoint, the path traversal vulnerability class, or the url.PathUnescape double-decode root cause described in the advisory
